description.A: Among the top 2000 most frequent Chechen words — the essential core vocabulary.
description.B: Appears in everyday and literary texts — active vocabulary of an adult native speaker.
description.C: Rare or specialised — present in the corpus, but infrequent.
description.unattested: This word is in the dictionary but wasn't found in our text corpus. Level not yet determined.

disclaimer.title: Levels are approximate
disclaimer.body: Levels are calculated automatically from word frequency in a text corpus (currently Wikipedia). We're expanding the corpus with literature, religious texts and spoken sources to improve accuracy.

features.title: Everything you need to work with the language
features.subtitle: Search, morphology and analysis tools in one place

bilingualSearch.name: Bilingual search

conjugation.name: Conjugation

phraseology.name: Phraseology
phraseology.desc: Search across set expressions and idioms from all 14 sources.

wordLevels.name: Word levels

meta.title: About — Dosham
meta.description: Dosham is a unified digital Chechen dictionary: morphological analysis, declension, conjugation and an API combined from 14 sources.

hero.subtitle: A unified digital dictionary of the Chechen language with morphological analysis, declension, conjugation and a developer API.

problems.title: Why this matters
problems.description: Chechen has rich morphology — 4 noun classes, 8 cases and an ergative alignment — yet there has been no unified digital dictionary. The data is scattered across dozens of sources of different formats and coverage.

items.noSingleDictionary: No unified digital dictionary of Chechen
items.noApi: No API for developers of language apps

bilingualSearch.name: Bilingual search
bilingualSearch.desc: Chechen → Russian and Russian → Chechen with morphology-aware lemmatization.

declensionConjugation.name: Declension and conjugation
declensionConjugation.desc: 8 cases × 2 numbers for nouns. 9 tense forms, participles and negation for verbs.

phraseology.name: Phraseology and citations
phraseology.desc: Set expressions and literary citations from every source.

cefr.name: CEFR levels
cefr.desc: Every entry is tagged A1 through C2 for easier learning.

favoritesHistory.name: Favorites and history
favoritesHistory.desc: Save words and revisit your past queries.

api.name: REST API
api.desc: A full API with documentation for integration into third-party apps.

grammar.title: Chechen grammar
grammar.description: Dosham automatically detects declension type and generates full paradigms for every word.

segments.text: Nominative, genitive, dative, ergative, instrumental, substantive, locative, comparative
Present, perfective past, remote past, imperfective, future possible and factual, and more.

sources.title: Sources
sources.description: Combined data from 14 dictionaries — from general-purpose to specialized.

cta.title: Start using it
cta.text: Search works without registration. Sign up to save words and propose edits.

notice.title: Wire up MaxMind GeoLite2 to enable geolocation
notice.text: The {endpoint} endpoint is ready, but the {field} field on events is still empty. Once GeoIP is configured, all new events will be enriched automatically — historical events will not be backfilled.

1.title: Sign up at MaxMind
1.desc: Create a free account at {link} — no card required, takes 2 minutes.

2.title: Download GeoLite2-Country
2.desc: Account → Download Databases → GeoLite2 Country (MMDB Binary, ~6 MB). Place the file in {path}.

3.title: Install the dependency
3.desc: Add the package to the backend:

5.title: Done

meta.title: Dictionary merge — Pipeline — Admin — Dosham
meta.description: Step-by-step merge of parsed dictionaries into a single unified.json with dedup and enrichment

header.title: Dictionary merge
header.subtitle: Step-by-step merge of parsed dictionaries into a single unified.json with deduplication and entry enrichment.

nextRec.label: Recommended next step
nextRec.allDone: All dictionaries merged — unified.json is ready

dedup.title: Deduplication algorithm
dedup.desc: Entries are merged by the composite key: {keys}. If an entry with this key already exists in unified, it is enriched (new meanings, examples and sources are added). Otherwise it is added as new.

unifiedFile.title: Unified file
unifiedFile.notCreated: File not created yet — run the first merge

timeline.title: Merge log
timeline.empty: Log is empty — run the first merge

confirm.title: Run merge?
confirm.body: Dictionary {slug} will be merged into unified.json. A snapshot will be created first.

rollbackConfirm.title: Rollback to step {step}?
rollbackConfirm.body: unified.json will be restored from the snapshot. All steps after {step} will be removed from the log.

result.template: ✓ step: {step} · slug: {slug} · entriesFromDict: {fromDict} · newWords: {newWords} · enrichedWords: {enriched} · totalUnifiedEntries: {total} · nextRecommended: {next}

meta.title: Load into DB — Pipeline — Admin — Dosham
meta.description: Load unified.json into PostgreSQL, rebuild GIN index and estimate CEFR levels

header.title: Load into database
header.subtitle: Load unified.json into PostgreSQL, rebuild the GIN index and run a heuristic CEFR-level estimation.

action.title: Load unified.json → PostgreSQL
action.desc: All records from unified.json will be loaded into the entries table. Existing records will be updated (upsert). The GIN index will be rebuilt automatically.

skipped.title: Skipped entries
skipped.emptyTitle: Run a load to see skipped entries

history.title: Load history
history.empty: History is empty — no loads yet

confirm.title: Load into database?
confirm.body: {count} records from unified.json will be loaded into PostgreSQL. Existing records will be upserted, the GIN index will be rebuilt. This may take 10–15 seconds.
confirm.bodyNoCount: Records from unified.json will be loaded into PostgreSQL. Existing records will be upserted, the GIN index will be rebuilt.

meta.title: Data cleanup — Pipeline — Admin — Dosham
meta.description: Automatic entry cleanup: removing empty values, fixing broken examples, style normalization and deduplication

header.title: Data cleanup
header.subtitle: Automatic entry cleanup: removing empty values, fixing broken examples, normalizing style labels and removing duplicates inside entries.

action.title: Run data cleanup
action.description: Will automatically clean empty values, fix broken examples, normalize style labels and remove duplicate meanings inside entries.

normalizeStyles.title: Normalize styles
normalizeStyles.description: Unify style labels to a single format: colloq., obsolete, bookish, special, etc.

fixExamples.title: Fix examples
fixExamples.description: Repair broken encoding, remove OCR artifacts, normalize punctuation.

removeEmpty.title: Remove empty
removeEmpty.description: Remove meanings without text, empty strings in examples and phraseology arrays.

normalizeWords.title: Normalize words
normalizeWords.description: Trim whitespace, Unicode (NFC) normalization, remove invisible characters from word.

trimLong.title: Trim long fields
trimLong.description: Truncate excessively long values (> 2000 chars) and citations (> 500 chars).

dedupMeanings.title: Dedup meanings
dedupMeanings.description: Remove duplicate meanings inside one entry (exact text match).

history.title: Cleanup history
history.empty: History is empty — no runs yet.

confirm.title: Run data cleanup?
confirm.text: Automatic cleanup will run across all {count} entries: style normalization, fixing broken examples, removing empty values and deduplication of meanings.

meta.title: Rollback — Pipeline — Admin — Dosham
meta.description: Roll unified.json back to one of the previous snapshots

header.title: Pipeline rollback
header.subtitle: Roll unified.json back to one of the previous snapshots. Pick the step you want to return to — all later steps will be discarded.

pipelineRollback.warning: Rollback is a destructive operation. unified.json will be replaced with the selected snapshot; every step after it will be lost. A reload into the database (load) will be required afterwards.

currentState.label: Current unified.json state
currentState.empty: No data — pipeline has been reset

action.idleTitle: Pick a step to roll back to
action.idleDesc: Click one of the snapshots below to select a rollback target.
action.selectedDesc: unified.json will be replaced with snapshot: {label}
action.doneDesc: Pick another snapshot to run a new rollback.

timeline.title: Snapshots
timeline.empty: No snapshots yet. Run unify to create them.

confirmRollback.title: Roll back to step {step}?
confirmRollback.text: unified.json will be replaced with snapshot "{label}" ({count} records). All subsequent steps will be lost. This cannot be undone.

danger.title: Full pipeline reset
danger.description: Completely deletes unified.json, every snapshot and the step log. Parsed files remain. After a reset you'll need to run the whole pipeline again: parse → unify → load.

confirmReset.title: Reset the whole pipeline?
confirmReset.text: This will delete unified.json, all snapshots and the step log. Parsed files will be kept. This action is irreversible.

result.rollbackOk: ✓ Rollback complete: unified.json → step {step} ({label}) · {count} records. A reload into the database (load) is required.
result.rollbackToZero: ✓ Rolled back to empty state. Run parse → unify → load again.
result.resetOk: 💥 Pipeline has been reset. unified.json, all snapshots and the log have been removed. Run parse → unify → load again.

pipelineRollback.busy: Pipeline is busy — rollback and reset are temporarily unavailable

meta.title: Reset — Pipeline — Admin — Dosham
meta.description: Irreversibly delete unified.json, all snapshots and the step log

header.title: Full pipeline reset
header.subtitle: Irreversibly delete unified.json, all snapshots and the step log. Parsed files and the database are left untouched.

alternative.title: Maybe you want a rollback, not a reset?
alternative.description: Rollback lets you return to a specific step without losing snapshots.

dangerPanel.title: This action cannot be undone
dangerPanel.subtitle: After a reset you'll need to rebuild the whole pipeline: parse → unify-step → load → improve. This may take a significant amount of time.

unified.title: unified.json — deleted
unified.description: Single dictionary file with all merged entries

snapshots.title: Snapshots — deleted
snapshots.description: All restore points for each merge step

log.title: Step log — cleared
log.description: History of all merge operations (steps, stats)

parsed.title: Parsed files — preserved
parsed.description: All JSON files in data/parsed/ stay untouched

db.title: Database — untouched
db.description: PostgreSQL with entries is left as-is

sources.title: Source dictionaries — preserved
sources.description: Original dictionary files used for parsing

confirm.label: Type {phrase} in the field below to confirm

finalModal.title: Final warning
finalModal.body: You're about to completely delete unified.json ({entries} entries), {snapshots} snapshots and the step log. This action cannot be undone.

result.success: 💥 Pipeline reset. Deleted: unified.json ({entries} entries), {snapshots} snapshots, {freed} freed. Parsed files and DB preserved. To recover, run: parse → unify-step → load.

busy.title: Pipeline is busy
busy.description: Wait for "{operation}" to finish and try again.
